strategy: Pack Indemnity for your Feint. Any single trap you place makes him Ice Cleanse Ward every trap off himself and his minions, and Mass Feint triggers it too; Indemnity-protect the Feint so it sticks.
Avoid plain damage-over-time hits. Casting a DoT triggers Repel, which moves your DoT onto a random wizard. Use direct-damage spells instead.
Exploit Repel: after his Round-5 Power Link puts a heal-over-time on himself, a DoT can bounce that heal onto a wizard, giving you free healing.
Expect Freeze (80%) after his Ice natural attack or Smoke Screen (-45%) after his Fire one, so carry accuracy insurance or expect a fizzle.
Blade up and finish with a non-Fire, non-Ice school (he only resists those 15%) before Power Link out-heals you.
cheatNotes: He shields himself with a protected Mander Armor on Round 1; strip it with a manipulation spell or just blade through it.
After his Ice natural attack the whole team gets Freeze (80%) accuracy debuff, so your next spell may fizzle. Carry accuracy or expect a miss.
After his Fire natural attack you all get Smoke Screen (-45%) accuracy; same problem, plan for a possible fizzle.
Every fifth round he heals with Power Link (75+375), and with 75% outgoing healing he mends fast, so time your kill or use an infection.
Any single trap you place makes him Ice Cleanse Ward, wiping all traps off himself and his minions. Protect your Feint with Indemnity.
Mass Feint counts as placing a trap and sets off the Cleanse Ward, so it needs Indemnity too.
Indemnity on your Feint stops the Cleanse Ward from removing it.
Casting a damage-over-time makes him Repel it onto a random wizard, so prefer direct hits over DoTs.
That same Repel can bounce his own Power Link heal-over-time onto a wizard, so cast a DoT after he Power Links to steal the heal.
